response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine geospatial passion (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second. However, the role demands modern C++ or Rust as the primary skill, and his commercial C++ experience is a brief stint ending in 2015 with no Rust at all; his GIS work is web mapping rather than routing tiles or navigation pipelines, and his recent trajectory (TypeScript/Node full-stack, AI tooling) moves away from systems programming. Worth a conversation only if the team can accept a C++ ramp-up.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience, including founding an open-source geo-data platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) in DLang at millions of HTTP requests per second
Long, broad engineering track record with strong testing and software-craftsmanship practices
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months ending 2015); no Rust experience
No routing, navigation-guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience — GIS background is web mapping, not routing data
Recent roles center on TypeScript/Node web development, suggesting drift away from systems-level work

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain experience (web GIS, not routing)
